title: Countries that are long-standing full democracies
subtitle: Data by the [Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy](#dod:regimes-lexical-index). Full democracies are political systems in which citizens have the right to choose the chief executive and the legislature in multi-party, uncertain elections, and enjoy freedoms of expression, assembly, and association.
caption:
Countries that are long-standing full democracies

Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy. Full democracies are political systems in which citizens have the right to choose the chief executive and the legislature in multi-party, uncertain elections, and enjoy freedoms of expression, assembly, and association.

This stacked area chart displays the share of countries classified as long-standing full democracies from 1789 to 2025. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 100%, while the horizontal axis marks years in intervals. The chart is divided into two primary sections: a large purple area labeled "Not full democracies" and a multi-colored section at the bottom representing "Full democracies." The full democracy section is further segmented by the duration of democratic status, with legend bands for countries aged 1-18 years (lightest yellow), 19-30 years (pale green), 31-60 years (teal), and 61-90 years (dark blue). From 1789 until approximately 1900, the chart shows almost exclusively the purple area, indicating a near-zero share of full democracies. A small emergence of the lightest yellow band appears around 1900, followed by a slight expansion and contraction before 1950. After 1950, the colored bands representing full democracies begin a sustained upward trend. By the year 2000, the combined height of the democracy bands reaches approximately 40% of the total chart area. The lightest yellow band, representing the newest democracies, forms the top layer of this cluster, while the dark blue band, representing the longest-standing democracies, sits at the base. Between 2000 and 2025, the total share of full democracies fluctuates slightly but remains in the range of 30% to 40%, with the purple area continuing to occupy the majority of the chart space, roughly 60% to 70%.
